t: Diaphragmatic breathing — first-line behavioral intervention (Chitkara D.K. et al. Am J Gastroenterol 2006; effect 50–70%).
In ID/ASD, functional behavior analysis and ABA.
Medical: GERD with proton pump inhibitor initial intervention.
NICE NG69 — general eating disorder principles.
Slow abdominal breathing for 5–10 minutes after eating — prevents regurgitation.
Habit Reversal Training (HRT — Habit Reversal Training)
Analysis of behavior trigger and maintainer.
Significant improvement with diaphragmatic breathing and habit reversal.
Myth 1: "Rumination is a form of GERD"
distinct disorder; manometry and clinical model differ.
Nissen fundoplication ineffective for functional rumination and contraindicated; behavioral intervention first-line.
It is a semi-voluntary behavior, based on conditioning; punishment is ineffective.
Myth 5: "Cannot be discontinued in a patient with ID/ASD"
Behavioral interventions are effective; active intervention is essential for nutritional complications.
Chitkara D.K., Van Tilburg M., Whitehead W.E., Talley N.J. Teaching diaphragmatic breathing for rumination syndrome. Am J Gastroenterol 2006;101(11):2449–2452.
Rome IV Diagnostic Criteria for Disorders of Gut-Brain Interaction. Rome Foundation; 2016.
